lever_summary: Wire the pass67 dense i64 storage into eval_comparison. The same-shape i64 compare reads both contiguous as_i64_slice() slices directly, and a new eval_i64_scalar_compare handles I64 scalar<->tensor in both operand orders (the generic scalar path ran the heavy compare_literals per element). Output is a Bool tensor (no dense Bool storage exists), so the win is on the input side.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage applied to comparisons
alien_graveyard.mapping: consume the contiguous i64 backing store (mcqr.30.13) in the comparison path instead of per-element tagged-union dispatch

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (vector_i64) inputs vs Vec<Literal>-backed reference benches run in the same process, isolating the data-model win from cross-worker CPU variance.

lt_scalar_64k_i64.note: headline: the generic scalar-broadcast path runs compare_literals (promote/literal_to_i128) per element; the dense path is a tight int_cmp fold

command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'lt_64k_i64|lt_scalar_64k_i64' --save-baseline i64cmp

change.lever: eval_same_shape_i64_compare gains an as_i64_slice() dense fast path; new eval_i64_scalar_compare wired into both Scalar(*)Tensor and Tensor(*)Scalar arms after the F64 scalar-compare attempt. Both fall back to the unchanged generic path when not dense i64.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: slices read in element order; scalar broadcast preserves operand order via scalar_on_left for the asymmetric ordered comparisons (Lt/Le/Gt/Ge)
isomorphism.comparison_op: unchanged: for I64xI64 compare_literals returns int_cmp(i128::from(a), i128::from(b)); the fast paths apply the same dispatcher int_cmp with the same i128 widening
isomorphism.output_dtype: Bool tensor, identical to the generic path (Bool has no dense storage; output stays Vec<Literal>)
isomorphism.errors: as_i64_slice() is Some only for I64 dense storage; mixed/Literal-backed/non-I64-scalar inputs return None and run the unchanged generic compare_literals loop

validation.name: i64_compare_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib comparison
validation.result: passed: 17 tests incl new dense_i64_compare_bit_identical_to_literal_path (same-shape + both scalar orders, all 6 comparisons, i64::MIN/MAX)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
